from dataclasses import dataclass
import datetime
import re

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to format ISO dates into a more readable form
    def _format_date(date_str: str) -> str:
        try:
            dt = datetime.datetime.strptime(date_str, "%Y-%m-%d")
            return dt.strftime("%d/%m/%Y")
        except ValueError:
            return date_str

    # Mapping from predicate to a formatting lambda (subject, object) -> phrase
    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes and whitespace)
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip().strip('\'"')

    predicate_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {_format_date(_clean_obj(o))}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {_clean_obj(o)} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        # Revised phrasing for clearer ownership / roles
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{add_article(_clean_obj(o))} is the coach of {s}",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s draft team is {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected in round {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s draft pick is {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} debuted with {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        # Additional predicates beyond the athlete domain
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{add_article(_clean_obj(o))} is the leader of {s}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{add_article(_clean_obj(o))} owns {s}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"{add_article(_clean_obj(o))} is the chairman of {s}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"{add_article(_clean_obj(o))} is the general manager of {s}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s stadium is {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s ground is located in {add_article(_clean_obj(o))}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an ethnic group of {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{add_article(_clean_obj(o))} is spoken in {s}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a UTC offset of {_clean_obj(o)}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the {_clean_obj(o)} time zone",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} is {_clean_obj(o)}",
    }

    # Build a quick lookup for manager of clubs (subject = club, predicate = manager)
    club_manager = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.predicate == "manager":
            club_manager[t.subject] = t.object

    # Group triples by subject to build a compact description per entity
    subject_map = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    # Helper to add a definite article where appropriate
    def add_article(obj: str) -> str:
        lowered = obj.lower()
        # Add 'the' for common entity types if not already present
        prefixes = ("club", "team", "university", "stadium", "ground", "league", "city", "country")
        if any(lowered.startswith(p) for p in prefixes):
            return obj
        # Simple heuristic: if the name contains words like 'Club', 'Team', 'University', prepend 'the '
        if any(word in obj for word in ("Club", "Team", "University", "Stadium", "Ground", "League", "City", "Country")):
            if not lowered.startswith("the "):
                return "the " + obj
        return obj

    # Updated predicate map to use the article helper where needed
    predicate_map.update({
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s draft team is {add_article(o)}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {add_article(o)}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {add_article(o)}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} played for {add_article(o)}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} made his debut for {add_article(o)}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {add_article(o)}",
    })

    def join_parts(parts):
        """Join a list of strings with commas and an 'and' before the last element."""
        if not parts:
            return ""
        if len(parts) == 1:
            return parts[0]
        return ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + " and " + parts[-1]

    # First, build a clause for each subject as before
    raw_clauses = {}
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_map.items():
        phrase_parts = []
        # Handle predicates that can have multiple objects, especially club/currentclub
        club_objs = []
        # Collect club and currentclub objects preserving order of appearance
        for pred, obj in pred_objs:
            if pred in {"club", "currentclub"}:
                club_objs.append(obj)
            else:
                phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, obj)
                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        if club_objs:
            if len(club_objs) == 1:
                # Single club – simple present phrasing
                phrase_parts.append(f"{subj} plays for {club_objs[0]}")
            else:
                # Multiple clubs – first ones are former, last is current
                former = ", ".join(club_objs[:-1])
                current = club_objs[-1]
                if len(club_objs) == 2:
                    phrase_parts.append(f"{subj} played for {former} and now plays for {current}")
                else:
                    phrase_parts.append(f"{subj} played for {former} and now plays for {current}")
        raw_clauses[subj] = join_parts(phrase_parts)

    # Merge clauses where an object is also a subject, embedding it as a relative clause
    merged_clauses = {}
    used_subjects = set()

    # Helper to strip leading subject phrase from a clause
    import re
    def strip_leading(subj, clause):
        # Remove patterns like "Subject is ...", "Subject was ...", "Subject plays ..."
        pattern = rf'^{re.escape(subj)} (is|was|plays|competes|belongs|has|are|were) '
        return re.sub(pattern, '', clause, flags=re.I)

    # Pre‑compute stripped descriptions for each subject
    stripped_desc = {subj: strip_leading(subj, clause) for subj, clause in raw_clauses.items()}

    for subj, clause in raw_clauses.items():
        # Find any object that is also a subject
        for obj_subj in raw_clauses.keys():
            if obj_subj == subj:
                continue
            # Use word boundaries to avoid partial matches
            pattern = rf'\b{re.escape(obj_subj)}\b'
            if re.search(pattern, clause):
                # Embed the object's description as a relative clause
                rel_clause = f"{obj_subj}, which {stripped_desc[obj_subj]}"
                clause = re.sub(pattern, rel_clause, clause)
                used_subjects.add(obj_subj)
        merged_clauses[subj] = clause

    # Collect clauses that have not been embedded elsewhere
    final_parts = [clause for subj, clause in merged_clauses.items() if subj not in used_subjects]

    if not final_parts:
        return ""
    final_text = join_parts(final_parts) + "."
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

    # First, build a clause for each subject as before
    raw_clauses = {}
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_map.items():
        phrase_parts = []
        # Handle predicates that can have multiple objects, especially club/currentclub
        club_objs = []
        # Collect club and currentclub objects preserving order of appearance
        for pred, obj in pred_objs:
            if pred in {"club", "currentclub"}:
                club_objs.append(obj)
            else:
                phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, obj)
                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        if club_objs:
            if len(club_objs) == 1:
                # Single club – simple present phrasing
                phrase_parts.append(f"{subj} plays for {club_objs[0]}")
            else:
                # Multiple clubs – first ones are former, last is current
                former = ", ".join(club_objs[:-1])
                current = club_objs[-1]
                if len(club_objs) == 2:
                    phrase_parts.append(f"{subj} played for {former} and now plays for {current}")
                else:
                    phrase_parts.append(f"{subj} played for {former} and now plays for {current}")
        raw_clauses[subj] = join_parts(phrase_parts)

    # Merge clauses where an object is also a subject, embedding it as a relative clause
    merged_clauses = {}
    used_subjects = set()

    # Helper to strip leading subject phrase from a clause
    import re
    def strip_leading(subj, clause):
        # Remove patterns like "Subject is ...", "Subject was ...", "Subject plays ..."
        pattern = rf'^{re.escape(subj)} (is|was|plays|competes|belongs|has|are|were) '
        return re.sub(pattern, '', clause, flags=re.I)

    # Pre‑compute stripped descriptions for each subject
    stripped_desc = {subj: strip_leading(subj, clause) for subj, clause in raw_clauses.items()}

    for subj, clause in raw_clauses.items():
        # Find any object that is also a subject
        for obj_subj in raw_clauses.keys():
            if obj_subj == subj:
                continue
            # Use word boundaries to avoid partial matches
            pattern = rf'\b{re.escape(obj_subj)}\b'
            if re.search(pattern, clause):
                # Embed the object's description as a relative clause
                rel_clause = f"{obj_subj}, which {stripped_desc[obj_subj]}"
                clause = re.sub(pattern, rel_clause, clause)
                used_subjects.add(obj_subj)
        merged_clauses[subj] = clause

    # Collect clauses that have not been embedded elsewhere
    final_parts = [clause for subj, clause in merged_clauses.items() if subj not in used_subjects]

    if not final_parts:
        return ""
    final_text = join_parts(final_parts) + "."
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
